name: Job Application
description: Perfect for applying to part-time or full-time positions with enthusiasm and qualifications.
prompt:
Based on this job description, create an engaging job application for {firstName} {lastName} that stands out:

**Opening Statement (2-3 sentences):**
- Express genuine excitement for the role and company
- Mention the specific position you're applying for
- Include a compelling reason why you're interested

**Why I'm Perfect for This Role (2-3 paragraphs):**
- **Relevant Skills & Experience:**
  - Match 3-4 key requirements from the job description
  - Provide specific examples of how you've used these skills
  - Quantify achievements where possible (e.g., "increased efficiency by 25%")
  
- **Cultural Fit & Work Style:**
  - Show how your work ethic aligns with their needs
  - Mention relevant soft skills (communication, teamwork, etc.)
  - Include any company research or values alignment
  
- **Unique Value Proposition:**
  - What makes you different from other candidates
  - How you can contribute beyond the basic requirements
  - Any special certifications or unique experiences

**Why This Company/Role:**
- Show you've researched the company
- Explain why this role excites you
- How it fits your career goals

**Call to Action:**
- Express eagerness to discuss the opportunity
- Suggest next steps (interview, call, etc.)
- Show flexibility and enthusiasm

**Formatting Tips:**
- Use bullet points for skills and achievements
- Keep paragraphs concise and impactful
- Total length: 250-350 words
- Make it easy to scan and remember
- Add proper spacing between sections
- NO markdown formatting - use plain text only
- IMPORTANT: Use {firstName} {lastName} throughout the application
- Sign with the freelancer's actual name: {firstName} {lastName}

Job Description: {jobDescription}

Freelancer Profile:
- Name: {firstName} {lastName}
- Profession: {profession}
- Skills: {skills}
- Experience: {description}

Create an application that shows genuine enthusiasm, relevant qualifications, and makes them excited to meet you. Use proper spacing and formatting. Always use the freelancer's real name, never use placeholders.

name: Engaging Questions
description: Get clients thinking and engaged with thoughtful questions about their project.
prompt:
Based on this job description, create an engaging proposal for {firstName} {lastName} that uses questions to demonstrate expertise:

**Project Acknowledgment (1 paragraph):**
- Show you understand the basic scope
- Acknowledge the complexity and importance
- Express genuine interest in helping them succeed

**Strategic Questions (3-4 questions):**
- **Business Context Questions:**
  - "What's the primary business goal behind this project?"
  - "Who are the end users and what problems are we solving?"
  
- **Technical & Scope Questions:**
  - "What's your timeline and budget considerations?"
  - "Are there any technical constraints or preferences?"
  
- **Success & Future Questions:**
  - "How will you measure the success of this project?"
  - "What's your vision for future iterations or scaling?"

**Why These Questions Matter:**
- Explain how understanding these details leads to better solutions
- Show your expertise through the questions you ask
- Demonstrate strategic thinking and attention to detail

**My Approach:**
- Brief overview of how you'll use their answers
- Your methodology for gathering requirements
- How you'll ensure alignment and success

**Next Steps:**
- Suggest a discovery call or meeting
- What you'll prepare based on their answers
- How to move forward together

**Formatting:**
- Use the questions as conversation starters
- Keep it conversational and engaging
- Total length: 200-250 words
- Make them excited to answer your questions
- Add proper spacing between sections
- NO markdown formatting - use plain text only
- IMPORTANT: Use {firstName} {lastName} throughout the proposal
- Sign with the freelancer's actual name: {firstName} {lastName}

Job Description: {jobDescription}

Freelancer Profile:
- Name: {firstName} {lastName}
- Profession: {profession}
- Skills: {skills}
- Experience: {description}
- Projects: {projects}

Create a proposal that makes them think, shows your expertise, and gets them excited to have a conversation. Use proper spacing and formatting. Always use the freelancer's real name, never use placeholders.

name: Detailed Estimate
description: Provide a comprehensive project estimate with clear breakdown and value proposition.
prompt:
Based on this job description, create a detailed project estimate proposal for {firstName} {lastName}:

**Project Understanding (1 paragraph):**
- Summarize what you understand they need
- Acknowledge the scope and complexity
- Show you've thought through the requirements

**Project Breakdown (3-4 phases):**
- **Phase 1: Discovery & Planning**
  - Requirements gathering and analysis
  - Project planning and architecture design
  - Timeline: [X] days, Investment: $[X]
  
- **Phase 2: Development & Implementation**
  - Core development and testing
  - Regular progress updates and feedback
  - Timeline: [X] days, Investment: $[X]
  
- **Phase 3: Testing & Deployment**
  - Quality assurance and testing
  - Deployment and launch support
  - Timeline: [X] days, Investment: $[X]
  
- **Phase 4: Post-Launch Support**
  - Bug fixes and minor adjustments
  - Documentation and training
  - Timeline: [X] days, Investment: $[X]

**Total Investment: $[X]**
**Total Timeline: [X] weeks**

**What's Included:**
- Clear deliverables for each phase
- Communication and progress updates
- Quality assurance and testing
- Post-launch support period
- Source code and documentation

**Why This Investment Makes Sense:**
- Risk mitigation through structured approach
- Quality assurance and testing included
- Ongoing support and maintenance
- Scalable and maintainable solution
- Long-term value vs. short-term cost

**Terms & Conditions:**
- Payment schedule (e.g., 50% upfront, 50% on completion)
- Change request policy
- Support and maintenance terms
- Intellectual property rights

**Next Steps:**
- How to get started
- What you need from them
- Timeline for project kickoff

**Formatting:**
- Use clear headings and bullet points
- Include realistic timelines and costs
- Professional but approachable tone
- Total length: 300-400 words
- Make the value clear and compelling
- Add proper spacing between sections
- NO markdown formatting - use plain text only
- IMPORTANT: Use {firstName} {lastName} throughout the proposal
- Sign with the freelancer's actual name: {firstName} {lastName}

Job Description: {jobDescription}

Freelancer Profile:
- Name: {firstName} {lastName}
- Profession: {profession}
- Skills: {skills}
- Experience: {description}
- Projects: {projects}

Create an estimate that shows professionalism, transparency, and clear value for their investment. Use proper spacing and formatting. Always use the freelancer's real name, never use placeholders.

name: Strategic Approach
description: Demonstrate your problem-solving methodology and strategic thinking.
prompt:
Based on this job description, create a strategic proposal for {firstName} {lastName} that showcases your approach:

**Project Analysis (1 paragraph):**
- Acknowledge the client's specific needs and challenges
- Show you understand the scope and complexity
- Identify key success factors

**My Strategic Approach (2-3 paragraphs):**
- **Phase 1: Discovery & Planning**
  - How you'll gather requirements and understand their business
  - Initial planning and timeline development
  
- **Phase 2: Execution & Development**
  - Your development methodology and tools
  - How you'll ensure quality and communication
  
- **Phase 3: Delivery & Support**
  - Testing and quality assurance process
  - Post-launch support and maintenance

**Why This Approach Works:**
- Highlight your relevant experience with similar projects
- Explain how your methodology reduces risks
- Show how you'll deliver value efficiently

**Next Steps:**
- Clear timeline and milestones
- What you need from them to get started
- How we'll communicate and collaborate

**Key Benefits:**
- Risk mitigation through structured approach
- Clear communication and transparency
- Proven methodology for successful delivery

**Formatting Requirements:**
- Use proper paragraph breaks with blank lines between sections
- Include bullet points using regular dashes (-)
- Add spacing between major sections for readability
- NO markdown formatting - use plain text only
- Make it easy to scan and understand
- IMPORTANT: Use {firstName} {lastName} throughout the proposal
- Sign with the freelancer's actual name: {firstName} {lastName}

Job Description: {jobDescription}

Freelancer Profile:
- Name: {firstName} {lastName}
- Profession: {profession}
- Skills: {skills}
- Experience: {description}
- Projects: {projects}

Create a proposal that shows strategic thinking, clear methodology, and confidence in delivering results. Use proper spacing and formatting. Always use the freelancer's real name, never use placeholders.

name: Professional Cover Letter
description: A comprehensive cover letter that showcases your expertise and enthusiasm for the project.
prompt:
Based on this job description, create a professional and compelling cover letter for {firstName} {lastName} that follows this structure:

**Opening Paragraph (2-3 sentences):**
- Start with a strong, enthusiastic opening that shows genuine interest
- Mention the specific role/project you're applying for
- Include a brief personal connection or relevant experience

**Body Paragraphs (2-3 paragraphs):**
- **Experience & Skills:** Highlight 2-3 most relevant skills with specific examples
- **Project Understanding:** Show you understand their needs and challenges
- **Value Proposition:** Explain how your expertise will benefit their project
- **Past Success:** Include 1-2 brief examples of similar work (if applicable)

**Closing Paragraph (2-3 sentences):**
- Reiterate your enthusiasm and fit for the role
- Include a clear call-to-action
- End with a professional closing

**Tone Guidelines:**
- Professional yet warm and personable
- Confident but not arrogant
- Specific and detailed, not generic
- Show enthusiasm and genuine interest
- Keep it conversational and engaging

**Formatting Requirements:**
- Use proper paragraph breaks with blank lines between sections
- Include bullet points where appropriate using regular dashes (-)
- Keep total length to 200-300 words
- Make it scannable and easy to read
- NO markdown formatting - use plain text only
- Add proper spacing between paragraphs for readability
- IMPORTANT: Use {firstName} {lastName} throughout the cover letter
- Sign with the freelancer's actual name: {firstName} {lastName}

Job Description: {jobDescription}

Freelancer Profile:
- Name: {firstName} {lastName}
- Profession: {profession}
- Skills: {skills}
- Experience: {description}
- Projects: {projects}

Create a cover letter that feels personal, professional, and specifically tailored to this opportunity. Use proper spacing and formatting for easy reading. Always use the freelancer's real name, never use placeholders.

name: Concise Proposal
description: A short, impactful proposal perfect for smaller projects and quick responses.
prompt:
Based on this job description, create a concise and compelling proposal for {firstName} {lastName}:

**Quick Summary (1 sentence):**
- One-line overview of what you understand they need

**My Solution (2-3 bullet points):**
- **What I'll deliver:** Clear, specific deliverables
- **How I'll do it:** Brief methodology or approach
- **Why choose me:** 1-2 key differentiators

**Timeline & Investment:**
- Estimated timeline (be realistic)
- Rate or project cost (if applicable)
- Any special terms or conditions

**Why I'm the Right Choice:**
- 2-3 relevant skills or experiences
- 1 brief success story or achievement
- What makes you reliable and trustworthy

**Next Steps:**
- Clear call-to-action
- How to get started
- What you need from them

**Formatting:**
- Keep it under 150 words
- Use bullet points for clarity
- Make it scannable in 30 seconds
- Professional but friendly tone
- Add proper spacing between sections
- NO markdown formatting - use plain text only
- IMPORTANT: Use {firstName} {lastName} throughout the proposal
- Sign with the freelancer's actual name: {firstName} {lastName}

Job Description: {jobDescription}

Freelancer Profile:
- Name: {firstName} {lastName}
- Profession: {profession}
- Skills: {skills}
- Experience: {description}
- Projects: {projects}

Create a proposal that's short, sweet, and impossible to ignore. Every word should count! Use proper spacing and formatting. Always use the freelancer's real name, never use placeholders.
